description: Pre-seeded validator catalogs for tools/verify_baseline.py and tools/baseline_orchestrator.py. Each list is the initial seed; user is expected to tune (add/remove ids) as the orchestrator output reveals false-positives or misses.
H5_rationale: Question ids that must carry at least one scripture[] entry with figures including 'anthropomorphism' or 'anthropopathism'. Divine-attribute questions where the canon contains well-known anthropomorphic / anthropopathic passages (e.g. Gen 6:6, Ex 32:14, 1 Sam 15:11, Jonah 3:10, Jer 18:8, anthropomorphic 'hand of the LORD' / 'eyes of the LORD' passages, anthropopathic wrath/grief language). A subagent that produces a verdict on these without surfacing the figurative complicating texts is hiding the hermeneutical commitment. Validator failure mode: 'anthropomorphism-required-not-cited' if neither figure appears in any scripture[] entry AND the flag 'anthropomorphic-passages-omitted' is not set.
H6_rationale: Question ids where any verdict that affirms (in the dispensational direction) MUST carry the flag 'dispensational-lens-required' AND have 'dispensationalism' in evidence.hermeneutics.frameworks_in_play. These are eschatology questions where the verdict shifts based on dispensational vs covenantal vs historic-premillennial lens. The flag forces the dispensational commitment (a Brethren distinctive originating with Darby) to be visible, not smuggled. Validator failure mode: 'dispensational-lens-flag-missing' if affirms=true on these ids without the flag.
K2_rationale: Question ids where cult_marker_if_denied=true is plausibly defensible from apparatus + interlinear + concordance + pan-tradition consensus. These are Nicene-Chalcedonian-Apostolic foundations: Trinity boundaries, full deity+humanity of Christ (Chalcedon), bodily resurrection (Christ's and general), bodily second coming, virgin birth, eternal sonship, hypostatic union, anti-Pelagianism (canonical since Council of Carthage 418), Christ as exclusive way (pluralism denial), apostolic-canon-closure (post-apostolic new revelation rejected pan-tradition), basic monotheism (anti-polytheism, anti-Christian-Science docetism). The orchestrator MUST still verify each on its own per-question evidence — being on this list grants eligibility to set cult_marker_if_denied=true, NOT a free pass. Each question still needs (i) would_die_for=true, (ii) canonical demonstration via apparatus + interlinear + concordance, (iii) ≥6 distinct counter_witness traditions all with stance='affirms'. Validator failure mode: 'cult-marker-on-non-eligible-question' if cult_marker_if_denied=true on an id NOT in this list.
K2_excluded_rationale: Question ids that are tier=essential under the formation's calibration but FAIL the pan-tradition consensus test, and so cannot legitimately carry cult_marker_if_denied=true under the corrected bar. Reformed Solas (sola_fide, sola_gratia, solus_christus, imputed_righteousness, forensic justification) are Reformed-Lutheran-Anglican-Methodist consensus but Catholic + Orthodox + Anabaptist + Pentecostal frame justification differently — they fall under 'gospel-essential' (would_die_for=true) but not 'cult-grade' (cult_marker=false). Specific theories of atonement (penal substitution; broader substitutionary atonement) face similar Catholic/Orthodox divergence on the theory-of mechanism while affirming the fact-of. The Scripture-quality cluster (final_authority, inerrancy, infallibility, sufficiency, canon-66-books) faces real divergence: Catholic 73-book canon, Orthodox 76+, plus distinct Catholic / Orthodox positions on Scripture-tradition relationship (Dei Verbum). cult-icc-baptismal-regeneration is the cleanest example of bias-correction: Catholic and Orthodox AFFIRM baptismal regeneration; the denial-of-baptismal-regeneration position is Reformed-Baptist distinctive, so it cannot be cult-grade by pan-tradition consensus. het-rc-marian-dogmas and het-icon-veneration likewise: rejected by Reformed-Baptist substrate but AFFIRMED by Catholic and Orthodox — they're intramural disputes, not cult-markers. These ids may carry would_die_for=true (Ebenezer might be willing to die for them), but the cult-marker bar is canonical-pan-tradition, not formation-relative.
verifier_url_rationale: Two allow-lists (primary repositories + counter-witness primaries) and one deny-list. The verify_baseline.py URL check (--check urls) HEAD-requests every URL in evidence.web[] and reports failures. The deny-list is checked separately: any URL in evidence.web[] containing one of the forbidden domains is a hard validation failure (Reformed-aligned commentary sites that share the formation's substrate, plus brethrenarchive.org which would be defendant material in the baseline).
